recent_trades.summary: Exiting Chevron at a small loss to further reduce energy exposure and raise cash, as no near-term catalysts are evident and commodities remain under pressure.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oil prices have been sliding, and despite some positive sentiment headlines, our CVX position is -1.23% and lacks momentum. The sale frees up approximately $1,767, increasing cash to ~6.4% and simplifying the portfolio. We retain 42 trades to deploy later if better setups emerge.

recent_trades.reasoning: AMD has dropped to ~491 from 510 over the past month, creating a better entry. Multiple headlines highlight AMD's expanding role in AI hardware alongside NVIDIA. Adding from 1.6% to 3% — moderate conviction given the broader tech sector weakness in semis.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing GE Aerospace to an 8% weight based on bullish new analyst coverage and strong technical momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: GE Aerospace is up 0.51% over the past month and remains in a steady uptrend, validated today by fresh bullish analyst initiations. Expanding our position capitalizes on this persistent relative strength and positive sentiment.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Morgan Stanley following positive analyst upgrades and upward earnings revisions.
recent_trades.reasoning: Morgan Stanley shows a solid 30-day uptrend (+0.95%) and positive momentum today (+0.28%). Strong fundamental tailwinds from earnings revisions and price target upgrades make it an attractive entry point for financial sector exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing Intuitive Surgical to a 5% weight to unlock capital from a mildly lagging position.
recent_trades.reasoning: ISRG is currently down roughly 2% in the portfolio and has traded sideways over the last 30 days. Trimming this outsized 8.7% position helps balance our healthcare exposure while shifting funds to trending assets.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Oracle to a 5% portfolio weight to reallocate capital toward higher-momentum names in financials and industrials.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle has seen sluggish 30-day performance (-0.51%) and we currently hold a heavy 10% allocation. Reducing exposure frees up cash to fund Morgan Stanley and GE Aerospace, which are demonstrating stronger near-term catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming MA to 3% target after negative -0.08 sentiment and comparative underperformance versus V in fintech coverage.
recent_trades.reasoning: Position at 4.46% weight with flat 30D performance and headlines favoring peers like Visa; partial trim frees capital for higher-conviction healthcare names while respecting recent decision guidelines and avoiding full exit.

recent_trades.reasoning: Recent initiation brought it to ~2% but strong ongoing news flow and +1.0% intraday move justify scaling up; healthcare sector leadership with positive technical close near highs supports 5% allocation without concentration risk.

recent_trades.summary: Starting a modest XOM position as very strong energy sentiment and supportive analyst commentary pair with price near 30-day highs, confidence 7/10.

A moderate starter adds sector diversification and gives the portfolio exposure to a leadership area without overcommitting late in the morning.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to GE on bullish analyst initiation flow and steady industrial momentum near the upper end of its recent range, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: GE has positive sentiment, fresh analyst support, and a constructive technical setup with a gain over the last month while holding close to recent highs. Increasing this position modestly rotates capital from weaker semis into a name with both favorable news flow and steadier trend behavior.

recent_trades.summary: Cutting NVDA modestly again because momentum is still lagging despite ongoing AI enthusiasm and the latest commentary suggests buyers are hesitating, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NVDA remains below its 30-day high and the news tone is mixed, including a headline explicitly noting buyers stepping away even as AI infrastructure headlines stay active. Since it still has a place in the portfolio, I am not exiting, but I do want a smaller weight than stronger large-cap growth names until price action improves.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM further as it remains one of the weakest semiconductor holdings with neutral news and price sitting near the bottom of its 30-day range, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM is down roughly 3% over 30 days and trading just above its 30-day low, which is notably weaker than peers like AMD and the broader large-cap tech complex. News flow is not adding a fresh positive catalyst, and we already began reducing it earlier today; with capital constrained, I prefer to keep only a small residual allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 1.5% position in Broadcom on a bullish analyst price target of $630 and expanding AI infrastructure investment, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has declined 2.23% over the past month, presenting a pullback opportunity. A prominent analyst sees the stock reaching $630, supported by strong AI packaging bets. The news coincides with broader AI infrastructure momentum and demand signals from Nvidia's CFO. Position sizing is modest at 1.5% to manage risk given the stock's recent negative sentiment and high absolute price.

recent_trades.summary: Adding AVGO on the -2.2% pullback near 30-day lows with analyst target of $630 implying ~50% upside on AI infrastructure strength, confidence 7/10.

The stock has pulled back from $432.80 30-day high, creating a better entry. AI packaging expansion provides fundamental tailwind. Starting a modest 4% position given the near-term weakness.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to AMZN as it tests fresh 30-day highs and continues to show better momentum than most of the portfolio, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN is trading right at the top of its 30-day range near 270.73 and its intraday candles remain constructive, which supports building the position from a very small 0.51% weight. This is a measured add, not an aggressive one, because the position was initiated recently and I want to conserve trade budget.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming NVDA again as it remains below its recent highs and continues to lag the stronger mega-cap leaders, confidence 6/10.

recent_trades.reasoning: An expert projection of significant upside highlights AVGO's compelling AI infrastructure narrative. I am building a larger core position to capture this fundamental strength and capitalize on recent consolidation.

recent_trades.reasoning: Exxon Mobil is seeing highly positive sentiment (+0.61) following commentary that energy stocks present a strong value proposition compared to treasuries. Initiating a 4% weight introduces necessary energy exposure to the portfolio amid favorable macroeconomic tailwinds.

The current position is small at 3.41%, so increasing it to 5% improves exposure to a high-quality industrial with favorable relative strength without overconcentrating.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM as it remains one of the weakest chip names in the portfolio with neutral news and price action near the bottom of its 30-day range, confidence 7/10.

News flow is largely generic and sentiment is neutral, so I prefer to reduce exposure and recycle capital toward stronger names while keeping a smaller residual position.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Microsoft from 4.9% to 3.0% to normalize position size and free up cash, as the stock lacks near-term catalysts, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: At nearly 5%, MSFT is double our average position weight and has been flat. No immediate news or technical breakout justifies this concentration. Reducing to 3% brings it closer to equal-weight and releases ~$2,330 in cash for more actionable opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Costco from 3.7% to 2.5% ahead of its Q4 earnings report tomorrow, reducing event risk with the stock near highs, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Costco faces a high bar into earnings, and with the stock near all-time highs, a miss could trigger a sharp selloff. The 3.7% weight is outsized for a single event risk. Locking in partial gains and lowering to 2.5% protects capital and raises ~$1,420 in cash.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming EOG Resources from 3.4% to 2.5% to further reduce energy concentration as crude slides, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: EOG is directly correlated with oil prices, and the Iran deal progress threatens to push crude lower. After an earlier trim to 3.5%, it remains slightly above target. Reducing to 2.5% brings total energy exposure down to a more neutral level and adds ~$1,130 in cash.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Exxon Mobil from 3.9% to 2.5% to cut energy exposure as U.S. oil falls below $89 on Iran Hormuz deal hopes, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oil prices dropping below $89 on reports of a U.S.-Iran deal to reopen Hormuz shipping lanes creates near-term headwinds for energy stocks. Despite bullish long-term sentiment, the 3.9% weight is high for a sector facing a macro sell signal. Reducing to 2.5% aligns with equal-weight target and frees up ~$1,730 in cash.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in AbbVie driven by an analyst price target increase and strong pipeline progress.
recent_trades.reasoning: Piper Sandler raised its price target on AbbVie this morning, citing significant pipeline progress in the IBD space. Initiating a 5% starter position captures upside from these positive healthcare developments.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Broadcom to a 10% weight following the unveiling of the industry's first integrated Wi-Fi 8 and 5G platforms.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom's announcement of the world's first integrated 5G and Wi-Fi 8 FWA platform establishes a strong technological moat and a compelling near-term catalyst. Upsizing the position from 5% to 10% capitalizes on this fundamental strength.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Qualcomm position from 19% to 9% to reduce concentration risk and free up capital for fresh opportunities.
recent_trades.reasoning: Qualcomm has grown to a 19% portfolio weight, nearing the absolute concentration limit. With the stock slightly down today and lacking fresh positive catalysts compared to peers, reallocating half of this position improves portfolio diversification and frees up capital.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ORCL as it sits slightly underwater (-0.39%) with only neutral sentiment (+0.09) and no meaningful new catalysts today beyond the prior analyst upgrade.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL was initiated yesterday on an analyst upgrade but sentiment has cooled to near-neutral (+0.09) with no fresh headlines directly supporting it. The stock is marginally underwater at -0.39% and the analyst upgrade catalyst is now priced in. Freeing up this 2.59% capital for higher-conviction opportunities in defense and healthcare where we have fresh catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to RTX on confirmed $1.1B+ defense contract win alongside LMT, with very positive sentiment (+0.64) representing a new concrete catalyst beyond prior DARPA headlines.
recent_trades.reasoning: LMT/RTX won big US defense contracts worth over $1.1B per the latest headlines — this is a new, concrete catalyst distinct from the earlier DARPA story. RTX sentiment remains very positive at +0.64 and the stock has been near support. Increasing from 2.26% to ~5% weight. The defense spending environment remains robust and this contract win provides revenue visibility.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM from 6% to ~3% as the ByteDance catalyst has faded, stock remains -3.87% underwater with neutral sentiment and no new positive drivers today.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM was bought on the ByteDance AI chip deal but sentiment has turned neutral (-0.04) and the stock continues to underperform at -3.87% unrealized loss. The 30-day chart shows it near the 30-day low ($230.50 vs close $231.77). Reducing position to free capital for higher-conviction names while retaining a small position in case the ByteDance deal materializes.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to LLY from 10.1% to 12% — stock hitting 30-day highs with +0.5% today and positive sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY at 1085 is at its 30-day high, showing strong momentum. The GLP-1 narrative remains intact even with competitive concerns. +7.14% unrealized gain shows trend strength. Increasing to 12% while it has momentum.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

claude._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

gemini._pin_status: UNPINNED: Google echoes the alias unchanged. The adapter now captures response.model_version, so the real dated id will surface in model_id_returned on the next live run; pin from that.

deepseek._pin_note: Deliberately repointed deepseek-v4-flash -> deepseek-v4-pro on 2026-05-21: the deepseek-reasoner alias deprecates 2026-07-24 and maps to sub-frontier v4-flash; v4-pro is DeepSeek's current frontier reasoning tier, restoring cohort parity. No dated immutable snapshot exists beneath deepseek-v4-pro, so this is the most specific callable id (same standard as the dateless Claude ids); residual drift risk if DeepSeek repoints it. Runs in thinking mode at reasoning_effort=high (see deepseek_adapter.py).

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P -0.10%, Nasdaq -0.23%, Dow +0.39%. Consumer Discretionary led sectors (+1.71%) while Commodities lagged (-2.40%). Gemini 3.1 Pro led the day at +1.58%. 5 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek V4 Pro at +22.55%. Key headline: MU Stock Draws Investor Cheer After UBS’ New Price Target Implies A 100% Upside From Current Levels – What’s Driving The Analyst Optimism... — broadly positive for equities.
